items.title: Data centre builder lets slip plan to use irrigation water

children.content: Data centre builder Firmus Technologies posts to its website a statement, since removed, indicating its intention to get some of its cooling water from the state-owned irrigation system, adding "but we do not want to highlight this yet, as they are yet to agree".

items.title: Hobart prison sourcing meals from Victoria after floor fault shuts $19m kitchen

children.content: A newly built commercial kitchen at Hobart's Risdon Prison has shut just three months after officially opening due to construction faults, leaving staff to source daily meals for about 840 inmates from Victoria.

image.altText: Kitchen facility at Risdon prison, artist impression

items.title: Retired police officers lambast minister for trauma support delay

children.content: Tasmania's association of retired police officers has questioned the police minister over his commitment to the wellbeing of members after the stalling of a much-awaited trial that would offer trauma support.

image.altText: Man in suit next to police officer displaying taser

items.title: JackJumpers coach shares advice on how to win over Tasmanians

children.content: Being out amongst the people will be key for incoming Tasmania Devils coach Ken Hinkley, according to the man who led the state to NBL glory in 2024.

items.title: Map reveals Australia's 'pharmacy deserts' are not all in the bush

children.content: Australia's "pharmacy deserts" have been mapped for the first time, revealing remote NT communities and outer-city Melbourne have fewer pharmacies per person than some other parts of the country.

items.title: Albanese's career mirrors Howard's in two important ways

children.content: Anthony Albanese has been in near-fatal vehicle accidents not once, but three times. Politically, his is also a story of survival against the odds.

items.title: Victorian teachers are going on strike again — here's what you need to know

children.content: Victorian state school teachers and educators are going ahead with their second 24-hour strike this year after union members rejected the government's latest pay offer.

items.title: 'Irresponsible': Russia conducts live-fire weapons exercise off British coast

children.content: A UK minister has confirmed that a Russian naval vessel conducted a "live-fire weapons exercise" in international waters off the coast of Plymouth in south-west England.

items.title: Who paid for Pauline Hanson's trip to Europe? Not taxpayers, she says

children.content: In her first appearance since returning to Australia, Pauline Hanson fields questions about her trip to Europe with billionaire Gina Rinehart.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson sits on stage talking during a question and answer session at a breakfact function, against a blue background.

items.title: AI companies 'stole' Australian culture, news bosses say

children.content: The heads of the ABC, SBS, Nine and News Corp say AI companies should strike licensing deals rather than freely train on Australian news, warning journalism is a public good that must be protected.

image.altText: Hamish MacDonald,  Allesandra Galloni, Hugh Marks, Michael Miller, Jane Palfreyman, Matt Stanton.

items.title: Stingers hitting new heights thanks to shared love for each other

children.content: Australia's women's water polo team, the Stingers, were the most successful Australian team at the Paris Olympics, winning a stunning silver medal. This is the secret to their success.

children.content: The Iran war has been a contributing factor to Queensland's Triple Zero phone system being delayed until next year, the ABC understands.

items.title: Falling objects at BlueScope trigger breaches months after death

children.content: There have been six incidents involving falling tools and building materials at BlueScope, less than a year since contractor Jack McGrath was killed.

items.title: Sixteen-year-old in custody suspected of killing mother, injuring little sister

children.content: Police say a teenage boy is suspected to have stabbed his mother to death and seriously injured his 12-year-old sister in an alleged family violence homicide in Perth's south-east.

items.title: Protests turn violent after man dies during Italian police arrest

items.title: Armed with axes and a machete, ICE protester 'attacked' US federal building, FBI says

children.content: A former US Army soldier protesting Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) has been arrested in New York after allegedly setting off fireworks, lighting a fire and shooting a pellet gun outside a federal office building.

items.title: Former Catholic bishop sexually abused boys at 'shirts-off' parties, court hears

children.content: One of Australia's longest serving senior Catholic clerics had a sexual interest in young Aboriginal men and abused them in Western Australia's far north over several years, a Perth court hears.

items.title: Indonesia sentences leader of baby-trafficking ring to seven years' jail

items.title: India steps up response to US, Iran deadly ship strikes

children.content: The Indian government is taking action after 10 sailors were killed and dozens more injured in attacks on commercial cargo ships and oil tankers during the battle for control of the Strait of Hormuz.
